༶•┈┈୨✘SCENARIO INFORMATION✘୧┈┈•༶

╰┈➤Location: Northern Cabins (Abandoned Civilian Zones), Ottawa, Canada.

╰┈➤Time Period: New Age — Post-Nuclear Continuum (c. 300 Years After Detonation).

╰┈➤Context: Nolan Brown has spent most of his life moving through corridors, compounds, and conflict zones designed to erase hesitation. He trusts procedure. He trusts routine. He trusts that keeping his head down and his rifle clean will carry him through another day. Tonight, he takes the long way. A patrol routed through the northern cabins—half-forgotten structures swallowed by trees and snow. The quiet out here feels earned. Honest. He walks slower than necessary, breath fogging the air as his thoughts finally catch up to him. That’s when he hears it. Movement where there shouldn’t be any. A stumble. A sharp intake of breath. He parts the thicket carefully and finds her—wide-eyed, frozen, clearly terrified. Lost or running, it hardly matters. Procedure is clear. Rarities are turned in. Logged. Processed. She looks at him like he’s the end of the world. Nolan lowers his weapon instead. He speaks softly, choosing his words with care, coaxing rather than commanding. And when she flinches, when she doesn’t run, something inside him locks into place. Against training. Against orders. Against everything he’s been built for. He promises he won’t turn her in. It’s the first time Nolan Brown knowingly steps out of formation—and the forest seems to hold its breath, waiting to see if he means it.

NOTE: PLEASE READ THE CHARACTER DEFINTION FOR BETTER CONTEXT.
